task.text: A retried refund.succeeded webhook can credit a customer twice if the first delivery commits the ledger entry but times out before the idempotency marker. Make handling exactly-once at the application boundary without changing the public API.
successCriteria: restart after marker timeout does not re-credit
public RefundSucceeded event shape is unchanged

review.summary: {"status":"failed","findings":[{"severity":"high","file":"src/webhooks/refund-handler.ts:15","message":"Each RefundHandler creates a fresh in-memory transaction state. After ledger.credit succeeds but idempotency.mark fails, recreating the handler loses the committed marker and a retry credits the ledger again. The pinned atomicity test passes only because it manually shares MemoryTransactionState, which RefundHandler cannot do."},{"severity":"high","file":"src/webhooks/refund-handler.ts:15","message":"Duplicate serialization is scoped to one handler instance. Concurrent deliveries handled by separate RefundHandler instances have separate inflight maps, can both observe idempotency.has(event.id) as false, and can both credit. Thus the boundary does not provide the stated atomic transaction semantics for the supplied Ledger and IdempotencyStore."}]}

limitations: Requested model was not independently confirmed by an effective-model or reroute event.
Baseline validity requires the Kerno plugin to be absent from the App Server profile; verify the run preflight.
